# Wire — Cmd — command value

> **Feature delivered — acceptance criteria.** Generated from the as-built BDD tests in
> [`test/stories/wire_pipe_command_value_story_test.exs`](../../../echo/apps/echo_mq/test/stories/wire_pipe_command_value_story_test.exs). Every scenario below is a passing
> ExUnit test driving the real `EchoMQ` surface against Valkey, so the catalogue cannot
> drift from the code.

**4 scenarios.**

## Scenario: a flagged GET command value runs byte-identically to the bare verb, via run/2 and via Pipe.command/2

- **Given** a seeded value and a built, readonly-flagged GET command value
- **When** the command is run three ways: Cmd.run/2, Pipe.command/2, and the bare Pipe verb
- **Then** all three return the identical reply — the advisory flag never reached the wire

## Scenario: a built SET-with-TTL command value writes exactly as the bare verb (a write, advisory)

- **Given** a built SET command value with a 60s TTL — flagged a write
- **When** the SET command runs via Cmd.run/2, then the value + TTL are read back
- **Then** the write acknowledges and the bare-verb equivalent would have produced the same state

## Scenario: a list of built command values runs in one flush, replies in order (run/2 over a batch)

- **Given** three built command values: SET, GET, INCR on a sibling counter
- **When** the batch runs through Cmd.run/2 in one flush
- **Then** the replies map one-to-one to the command order

## Scenario: an un-curated verb rides Command.raw/1 and runs wire-equivalently to a curated build

- **Given** a seeded value and a raw GET command value
- **When** both the raw value and the curated value run via Cmd.run/2
- **Then** the raw and curated forms return the identical reply (the curated set is never a ceiling)
